items.title: Conversational link and memory-only Socratic seed
evidence: PR #7 is the sole active implementation candidate at 82a18716e6c758762916657f6577d78290f7b779
PR #7 implementation head 6a820c3b970f59f6d90e4154382a41bfe2 is not the current candidate; current implementation evidence is anchored at 6a820c3b970f59dc51d484cbb457de65819f3a7a
implementer evidence: 43 package tests passed before the focused Claude eligibility patch
implementer evidence: focused eligibility checks deny installed-only Claude account access and preserve authenticated-account versus API-key-backed paths
implementer evidence: workframe-0.2.1.tgz clean-install npm-bin smoke checks passed before the focused eligibility patch
PR #7 exact-head CI run 29356756330 failed at Harness verify; downstream package-install and negative-install steps were skipped
PR #9 was rejected and closed unmerged; its exact parser and Windows cancellation failures remain mandatory regressions
no live provider call, package publication, installation mutation, or non-CLI code change
acceptance: select one existing inference path conversationally without guessing ambiguity
represent account-backed and API-key-backed paths separately and disclose exact payer, credential source, and invocation before consent
require separate explicit consent before one minimal cancellable verification call
remove Docker, SSH, unrelated credentials, and other ambient authority handles from inference children
after a verified path, collect preferred name and first objective
print a bounded memory-only mirror without filesystem or installation mutation
package-local, real-PTY, Windows-semantics, exact-head CI, and packed-artifact checks pass without live credentials

failures: questioning or qualified language could authorize a provider call
authority-boundary tests did not exercise the complete flow
EOF and interruption were not handled deterministically
prompt echo and refusal text could falsely verify providers
best/default questions could select a candidate
verification was not cancellable after consent
account and API-key billing paths were conflated
inference children inherited Docker and SSH authority handles
the implementation branch was behind and diverged from main
no package or repository CI run was attached to the exact reviewed head
descriptive and negative-context candidate mentions can authorize a path
multi-clause exclusions can silently select an unrelated remaining candidate
Windows production taskkill completion and child-tree termination are not observed
exact-head CI is red and does not independently execute the package-local and packed-tarball matrix

required_regressions: descriptive and negative-context candidate mentions remain unresolved
mixed positive and negative clauses select only an explicit affirmative candidate and never an unrelated remainder
Windows production taskkill completion and runtime-child-tree exit are observed before cancellation settles
package-local and packed-tarball checks run against the exact review head

reported_repairs: installed-only Claude does not become an authenticated account-backed candidate
authenticated-account and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Claude paths remain separate
verification cancellation reaches HTTP and asynchronous child-process interfaces
inference children exclude Docker, SSH, unrelated credentials, and arbitrary ambient variables
hedged selection and exact structured-response checks have focused regressions
review_entry_requirements: rebase or rebuild from current main while preserving canonical non-CLI ledger history
carry all four completed review failure sets forward as regressions
run the full package suite including candidate-eligibility.test.js from one exact head
run packed-tarball, real-PTY, production Windows command semantics, and available repository CI from that same head
return WF-CLI-001 to review only with exact evidence; do not start WF-CLI-002

items.title: Truthful runtime and provider capability graph
Explicit apply and rollback authority gates

invariants: Do not modify packages/create-workframe, apps, services, infra, or existing runtime behavior.
Do not read, print, persist, or transmit credential values except directly to the explicitly selected provider after consent.
Do not overwrite, adopt, or mutate an existing setup without explicit approval of a concrete immutable plan.
Do not use a Workframe-hosted or implicit cloud fallback.
Publication remains a separate reviewed release decision.
